allies.rationale_text: Systems-thinking helps black-swan-events inspect interdependencies and failure propagation instead of treating surprise as an isolated shock.
Resilience turns black-swan awareness into preparation by focusing on vulnerability reduction and the ability to keep functioning under surprise.
Checklists support black-swan readiness when robust execution discipline is needed to close the gap between strategy and the unexpected obstacle.
allies.source_quote: structured around checklists and disciplined execution frameworks

antagonists.rationale_text: Anchoring works against black-swan caution when it causes people to underestimate the overall failure probability of fragile complex systems.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Black-swan-events conflicts with hindsight-bias when retrospective narratives make rare, genuinely unpredictable events appear to have been foreseeable, corrupting organizational learning about tail-risk exposure.
structured_tensions.source_quote: Scenario-analysis and hindsight-bias are source-real but remain omitted because they are not current target ids in the curated Wave 3 surface.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Black-swan-events conflicts with anchoring when people anchor on low component failure rates and underestimate whole-system fragility.
structured_tensions.source_quote: people tend to underestimate the total probability of failure

curation_notes.summary: The retained relation surface stays conservative around fragility, readiness, and the explicit current-vocabulary conflict with anchoring.
donor_drops: Did not preserve broader uncertainty neighborhoods outside the current curated vocabulary.
open_questions: Hindsight-bias wired in Task 3.3 sweep via new canonical model.
Scenario-analysis remains deferred — no canonical model id.
